file.content:
# From: https://github.com/caarlos0/dotfiles/blob/f9d985c3e8817f8fca133d908a910f173ea933d6/modules/tmux/tmux.conf
# License: MIT

# Color Mode
set -g default-terminal "screen-256color"
setenv -g COLORTERM "truecolor"

set -s escape-time 0
setw -g mouse on

# Fix SSH agent after reconnecting
# https://blog.testdouble.com/posts/2016-11-18-reconciling-tmux-and-ssh-agent-forwarding/
set -g update-environment "DISPLAY SSH_ASKPASS SSH_AGENT_PID SSH_CONNECTION WINDOWID XAUTHORITY WAYLAND_DISPLAY SWAYSOCK"
setenv -g SSH_AUTH_SOCK $HOME/.ssh/ssh_auth_sock

# Ensure window index numbers get reordered on delete.
set-option -g renumber-windows on

# Auto rename tmux window to current cwd
set-option -g status-interval 1
set-option -g automatic-rename on
set-option -g automatic-rename-format '#{b:pane_current_path}'

# Vim-mode copy/paste
set-window-option -g mode-keys vi
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi r send-keys -X rectangle-toggle
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'

# Open splits and windows in the current folder
bind u split-window -l 30% -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind i split-window -l 40% -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind c new-window -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Repeat time
set-option -g repeat-time 1000

# Clipboard
set -g set-clipboard on

# Status bar
set -g status "on"
set -g status-justify "left"
set -g status-left-length "100"
set -g status-right-length "100"
set -g status-left ""
set -g status-left-style NONE
set -g status-right-style NONE

# Reload tmux config
bind t source-file ~/.config/tmux/tmux.conf

# Default shell
set -gu default-command
set -g default-shell "$SHELL"

# Rename session and window
bind r command-prompt -I "#{window_name}" "rename-window '%%'"
bind R command-prompt -I "#{session_name}" "rename-session '%%'"

# Copy text on select
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "reattach-to-user-namespace pbcopy"

# Switch window
bind , previous-window
bind . next-window

# Move pane bindings
bind-key h   select-pane -L
bind-key j   select-pane -D
bind-key k   select-pane -U
bind-key l   select-pane -R

# Swap window bindings
bind-key -r "<" swap-window -d -t -1
bind-key -r ">" swap-window -d -t +1

# Resize pane bindings
bind-key -r H resize-pane -L 5
bind-key -r J resize-pane -D 5
bind-key -r K resize-pane -U 5
bind-key -r L resize-pane -R 5

# Split pane bindings
bind-key "|" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "\\" split-window -fh -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "-" split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "_" split-window -fv -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "%" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key '"' split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Yank pane history to clipboard
bind y run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -pS - | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Full pane history copied to clipboard"'
bind v run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -p | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Visible pane copied to clipboard"'

# Pane number indicator
set -g display-panes-colour colour233
set -g display-panes-active-colour colour245

# Clock mode
set -g clock-mode-colour colour39
set -g clock-mode-style 24

# =============================================================================
# Status bar — clean green theme
# =============================================================================
set -g status-style "bg=colour22,fg=colour255"
set -g status-left "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #S #[bg=colour22,fg=colour28] "
set -g status-right "#[fg=colour250]#{?#{pane_current_command},#{pane_current_command},} #[fg=colour28]| #[fg=colour255]%Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S "
set -g status-left-length 30
set -g status-right-length 60

# Window status
set -g window-status-format " #I:#W "
set -g window-status-current-format "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #I:#W "
set -g window-status-separator ""

# Pane border
set -g pane-border-style "fg=colour238"
set -g pane-active-border-style "fg=colour82,bold"

# Message style
set -g message-style "bg=colour28,fg=colour255"

# =============================================================================
# Plugin configuration
# =============================================================================
# Resurrect + Continuum (session persistence)
set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'
set -g @resurrect-processes 'btop fish git'
set -g @continuum-restore 'on'
set -g @continuum-save-interval '3'

# Tmux-thumbs (quick text copy)
set -g @thumbs-key Space

# Sessionx (session manager)
set -g @sessionx-bind 'o'

# Tmux sessionizer (fzf session picker)
bind S run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tss_function'"

# Last session (quick flip)
bind Tab switch-client -l

# Cross-session window picker
bind W run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tsw_function'"

# Pane content search (live + archived)
bind H run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tsh_function'"

# Kill sessions/windows via fzf
bind K run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tsk_function'"

# Extrakto (text extraction)
set -g @extrakto_key 'tab'

set -g history-limit 2147483647

# Persistent session history logger (starts once per server boot)
run-shell "pgrep -f 'session-logger.sh' > /dev/null 2>&1 || (nohup ~/.config/tmux/session-logger.sh > /dev/null 2>&1 &)"

content.thinking:
The user is asking about "tmux resurrecting on tpo (primary too)". I think they mean "tpo" might be a typo for "top" or they could mean the "primary" - but more likely they are asking about continuum auto-restore happening on the primary/main tmux server. 

Looking at the config:
- `set -g @continuum-restore 'on'` - this enables automatic restore
- `set -g @continuum-save-interval '3'` - saves every 3 minutes
- `set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'`

"tpo" might be short for "top" session/window, or they might mean something like the primary monitor, or "tpo" might be a typo for "too" as in "primary too" = "primary also".


I'm wondering if they're asking why resurrect is restoring sessions across multiple machines when they only expected it on one, or if "tpo" is actually referring to their primary machine and they're confused about why it's auto-restoring there. The `@continuum-restore 'on'` setting will automatically restore sessions whenever tmux starts, regardless of which machine it's running on—there's no built-in way to conditionally enable it only for specific machines. I should check if there's a NixOS or home-manager configuration that might be applying this selectively across different systems.

content.text: Let me check the NixOS/home-manager config to see if there's per-host configuration.

content.thinking:
Now I understand the setup. The user is asking why tmux-resurrect is running on their "tpo" (primary, too). 

The issue: `@continuum-restore 'on'` causes tmux-continuum to automatically restore the session on every new tmux server start. This is applied globally to all hosts since it's in the shared `tmux.conf`.

"tpo" is probably a typo for "top" - or maybe they mean the primary machine. Let me think about what "tpo" could mean:
1. Typo for "top" - the `top` session or window
2. Primary machine/host


3. Some specific session name

Actually, I think "tpo" + "(primary too)" suggests the user has multiple machines and this config applies to all of them including the primary. The `@continuum-restore 'on'` will restore sessions on every machine where this config is deployed. The `@resurrect-processes 'btop fish git'` setting means those specific processes get restored when the session comes back, which is why they're seeing resurrect activity on startup.

So the core issue is that `@continuum-restore 'on'` at line 131 enables automatic session restoration, combined with `@continuum-save-interval '3'` saving every 3 minutes, and since both the resurrect and continuum plugins are loaded in this shared config, they apply across all hosts. If they want this behavior limited to specific machines, they'd need to use host-conditional configuration in NixOS.

content.text:
The cause is `@continuum-restore 'on'` at line 131 of `tmux.conf`. This tells tmux-continuum to **automatically restore the last saved session on every tmux server start**, on any machine where this config is deployed.

Combined with:
- `@continuum-save-interval '3'` — auto-saves every 3 minutes
- `@resurrect-processes 'btop fish git'` — also restores those processes

Since `default.nix` loads the same `tmux.conf` with `resurrect` + `continuum` plugins for all hosts, restore happens on every machine including your primary.

**If you want to disable auto-restore on a specific host**, you'd need to conditionally set it in Nix, e.g.:

```nix
# In a per-host home-manager config
programs.tmux.extraConfig = ''
  set -g @continuum-restore 'off'
'';
```

Or if you just want to disable it globally, change line 131 to:

```
set -g @continuum-restore 'off'
```

You can still manually restore with `prefix + Ctrl-r` when you want it.

content.thinking:
Now I understand the structure. The user has these named hosts:
- `galactica` (darwin)
- `matic` (nixos)
- `kyber` (linux/homeConfigurations)

And generic hosts:
- `aarch64-darwin` 
- `x86_64-linux`
- Various runner configs

The user says "tpo" (which I think is "top" = the primary machine, likely `galactica` which is their main macOS machine) and "oar two" (sounds like "or two") - so they want continuum-restore only for specific non-primary hosts.


I'm parsing through what seems to be autocorrect issues in the original request. "Work in tmuxinator oar two not tpo" likely means they want continuum-restore enabled for two specific hosts but not their primary machine, though the exact host names are still unclear from the speech-to-text transcription.

The named hosts are `galactica`, `kyber`, and `matic`. "tpo" is likely a typo for "top" = `galactica` (primary macOS machine). "oar two" = "or two" - two specific hosts. Maybe they want resurrect enabled on `kyber` and `matic` but not `galactica`.

Actually, let me re-read the user's messages:
1. "why is it tmux resurrecting on tpo (primary too)" - they're surprised resurrect is running on "tpo" (their primary)
2. "no; only for primary tpo[Request interrupted by user]" - wait this was interrupted, so they might have been saying "no, only for primary too" or "not for primary tpo"


3. Looking at the latest message, they're saying resurrect should only be enabled for the work tmuxinator config on the secondary hosts (`kyber` and `matic`), not on their primary machine.

I could add a parameter to the tmux module to control this, or more simply just override the continuum restore binding in galactica's extra config. Let me check how galactica's home-manager configuration is structured.

Now I understand the architecture. The user wants:
- continuum-restore DISABLED for `galactica` (their primary macOS machine, "tpo" = "top")
- continuum-restore ENABLED for `kyber` and `matic` (the two secondary servers, "oar two" = "or two")

The simplest fix: in `galactica/default.nix`, override the tmux extraConfig to disable continuum-restore:

```nix
programs.tmux.extraConfig = lib.mkAfter ''
  set -g @continuum-restore 'off'
'';


```

This appends after the shared tmux.conf and overrides the `@continuum-restore 'on'` setting since tmux processes settings in order, with the last one taking precedence. Now I'll add this to `galactica/default.nix`.

content.text: Got it — disable `@continuum-restore` on `galactica` (primary), keep it on for `kyber` and `matic`. The simplest fix is to override it in `galactica/default.nix` with `mkAfter` so it appends after the shared `tmux.conf`:

content.content: The file /Users/shunkakinoki/dotfiles/named-hosts/galactica/default.nix has been updated successfully.

content.text: This works because tmux processes config top-to-bottom and the last `@continuum-restore` wins. `mkAfter` ensures this appends after the shared `tmux.conf` content. `kyber` and `matic` are unaffected and keep `'on'`.
